title: Extraction of copper(II) from sodium salicylate medium using neutral organo-phosphorus extractant Cyanex-923 in toluene
author: S. U. Gaikwad
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Siddharth College of Arts, Science and Commerce, Dr. D. N. Road, Fort, 

 Mumbai-400 00 l, India 

  E-mail :  sureshpawar_2004@rediffmail.com Fax : 91-22-22819049 

  Manuscript received 1 June 2009, revised 7 April 2010, accepted 13 April 1010  

 The neutral extractant Cyaitex-923 has been used for the extraction of Cu II  from sodium salicylate medium. This metal ion was found to be quantitatively extracted with Cyanex-923 in toluene at pH 6 and from the organic phase it is stripped with 3  M  HCI solution. The effect of pH, sodium salicylate concentration, reagent concentration, equilibration period, diluents, diverse ions and stripping agent on the extraction of Cu II  has been studied. The stoichiometry of the extracted species of this metal ion was determined on the basis of the slope analysis method. The reaction proceeds by solvation and the probable extracted species found was Cu(HSal) 2 . 2 Cyanex-923.
